vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1821: Linear responses never feed updateRateLimit — getBackoffMs('linear') is always 0, so pollers have zero rate-limit awareness

plan.title: Linear responses never feed updateRateLimit — pollers have zero rate-limit awareness
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: updateRateLimit('linear', …) is never called: the only call sites are GitHub (issue-data-service.ts:861, substrate-bug-poller.ts:94). The Linear fetch path (pollLinear → fetchLinearIssues, issue-data-service.ts:949/:1010) issues a raw fetch to api.linear.app/graphql (:1089) and discards response.headers, reading only the JSON body and throwing on json.errors (:1100). So getRateLimit('linear') is always null, getBackoffMs('linear') always returns 0 (cache-service.ts:337-339), and scheduleNext never lengthens the Linear interval. When Linear returns its 2,500-req/hr rate-limit error (PAN-1817 observed 84+ occurrences), pollLinear catches the throw, sets lastError, and reschedules at the base interval — an error loop, not a backoff. Quota exhaustion is also invisible to operators, so pan close / pan wipe tracker steps fail silently.
narratives.Proposal: Feed Linear rate-limit headers into updateRateLimit('linear', …) symmetrically with GitHub (parse on every response, before the error throw, with defensive seconds-vs-milliseconds reset normalization). Record exhaustion (remaining:0 + Retry-After) on 429/RATELIMITED. Add a tracker-generic getSuspensionMs(tracker) to CacheService and honor it in scheduleNext so an exhausted tracker suspends polling until its window resets (clamped to a 1-hour ceiling) then resumes automatically. Surface the state in the cache-status diagnostics (backoffMs / suspendedUntil / rateLimited) and add a pan doctor warn row. Backend-only; the frontend health strip stays in PAN-1817's scope.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Frontend dashboard health-strip / kanban visual for quota exhaustion (PAN-1817 ask #4 frontend scope)
- Reducing Linear request volume / GraphQL batching / per-issue fan-out elimination (PAN-1817 asks #1 & #3)
- Peer-dashboard cache-only behavior or zombie-stack teardown (PAN-1817)
- Rally-specific rate-limit header parsing (only the generic suspension mechanism applies if its row is ever populated)
- Migrating pollLinear/fetchLinearIssues onto the Effect linear-client.ts SDK path
- Changing getBackoffMs tier thresholds or the 5-minute intervals.max clamp

autoDecisions.summary: Scope limited to backend rate-limit awareness (poller backoff/suspension + doctor row + cache-status fields); frontend health strip deferred to PAN-1817 ask #4.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue title and asks are about poller awareness and a doctor/health signal; duplicating PAN-1817's UI would create drift.

autoDecisions.summary: Suspension mechanism made tracker-generic (getSuspensionMs + scheduleNext) rather than Linear-special-cased.
autoDecisions.rationale: GitHub already feeds updateRateLimit; a generic mechanism is simpler, has no behavioral downside (GitHub's 5k/hr never trips), and avoids a one-off Linear branch.

autoDecisions.summary: Linear reset timestamp normalized defensively: value < 1e12 treated as seconds (×1000), otherwise milliseconds.
autoDecisions.rationale: Cannot hit the live Linear API during planning without burning the very quota in question; the magnitude heuristic is correct for both GitHub-style (seconds) and Linear-style (ms) encodings, removing the guess.

autoDecisions.summary: No new SQLite table or schema migration; suspension derived from the existing rate_limits row (remaining ≤ 0 && reset_at > now).
autoDecisions.rationale: Those two persisted fields fully determine suspension; a new column/table would be dead weight.

items.title: Parse Linear rate-limit headers and feed updateRateLimit('linear', …)

narrative.Action: In fetchLinearIssues (issue-data-service.ts ~:1089-1102), after `await fetch(...)` resolves and BEFORE `response.json()` / the json.errors throw, parse Linear's rate-limit headers and call this.cache.updateRateLimit('linear', { remaining, total, resetAt }). Use header names x-ratelimit-requests-remaining / -limit / -reset, falling back to the bare x-ratelimit-* names if absent. Normalize the reset value defensively (D2): a value < 1e12 is Unix seconds (×1000), otherwise Unix ms. Extract/reuse a parseIntegerHeader(headers, name) helper (see substrate-bug-poller.ts:105-110) that returns null on missing/unparseable values; skip the update (never throw) when remaining or total is null. Mirror the GitHub callback at :854-862.

items.title: Given a Linear fetch response carrying x-ratelimit-requests-remaining/-limit/-reset, fetchLinearIssues calls cache.updateRateLimit('linear', …) so getRateLimit('linear') returns the parsed remaining/total/resetAt (verified by a unit test with a mocked fetch).
Given a reset header value in Unix seconds (~1.7e9) and one in Unix milliseconds (~1.7e12), normalization produces the same correct future ISO-8601 resetAt (values <1e12 treated as seconds).
When the rate-limit headers are missing or unparseable, fetchLinearIssues does NOT call updateRateLimit and does NOT throw from the header-parsing step — issue fetching proceeds unchanged.
Header parsing runs before the json.errors throw, so a response that both carries rate-limit headers and a GraphQL error still records the rate limit (verified by a test where json.errors is set).
Record quota exhaustion on Linear 429 / RATELIMITED before throwing

narrative.Action: In fetchLinearIssues, when response.status === 429 OR the GraphQL error indicates rate-limiting (message contains 'rate limit' or 'RATELIMITED', consistent with linear-client.ts:139), ensure 'linear' is recorded as exhausted via cache.updateRateLimit('linear', { remaining: 0, total, resetAt }) before the existing throw. If the rate-limit headers were absent (ac of linear-rl-headers recorded nothing), derive resetAt from the Retry-After header (now + seconds) or default to now + 3_600_000 ms. Preserve the existing thrown Error so pollLinear's catch still sets lastError.

items.title: Given a Linear response with HTTP status 429, fetchLinearIssues records updateRateLimit('linear', { remaining: 0, … }) and then throws (verified: getRateLimit('linear').remaining === 0 after the rejected call).
Given a 429 with no rate-limit headers but a Retry-After header of N seconds, the recorded resetAt equals now + N seconds (asserted with fake timers).

Given a GraphQL body whose error message contains 'RATELIMITED' (HTTP 200), fetchLinearIssues still records remaining:0 and throws, defaulting resetAt to now + 1 hour when no reset/Retry-After is present.
Add getSuspensionMs(tracker) to CacheService

narrative.Action: Add a pure method getSuspensionMs(tracker: string, now: number = Date.now()): number to CacheService (cache-service.ts). It reads getRateLimit(tracker); returns 0 when there is no limit row, when remaining > 0, when resetAt is unparseable, or when resetAt <= now; otherwise returns (resetMs - now). The `now` parameter makes it deterministically testable with fake timers. No schema change (NFR-3) — derived entirely from the existing rate_limits row. Callers are responsible for the 1-hour ceiling clamp (D4).

items.title: getSuspensionMs returns 0 when getRateLimit(tracker) is null, and 0 when remaining > 0 regardless of resetAt.
Given remaining === 0 and resetAt one hour in the future, getSuspensionMs returns approximately 3_600_000 (within tolerance, asserted with a fixed `now`).
Given remaining === 0 but resetAt already in the past (or unparseable), getSuspensionMs returns 0 (window already reset — no suspension).
Honor getSuspensionMs in scheduleNext to suspend exhausted trackers

narrative.Action: In scheduleNext (issue-data-service.ts:570-598), after computing effectiveInterval, call this.cache.getSuspensionMs(tracker). When > 0, set the next-poll delay to Math.min(suspendMs, 3_600_000) (D4 ceiling), bypassing the intervals.max clamp, log a warn line, and store it on state.currentInterval. When 0, use the existing effectiveInterval unchanged (NFR-1: non-exhausted cadence untouched). The setTimeout callback body (poll dispatch + reschedule) is unchanged.

items.title: Given a tracker whose getSuspensionMs returns 120_000, scheduleNext arms its timer for ~120_000 ms instead of the base interval (asserted with fake timers + a spied setTimeout or by advancing time and observing the poll fires only after the suspension).
Given getSuspensionMs returns a value above 3_600_000, the armed delay is clamped to 3_600_000 (D4 ceiling).
Given getSuspensionMs returns 0, scheduleNext arms the timer at the existing effectiveInterval (clamped to [min,max]) — GitHub/Rally/normal-Linear cadence is unchanged (NFR-1).
After a suspension delay elapses and the window resets (remaining restored), the next scheduleNext call returns to the normal interval — polling resumes automatically with no manual intervention.
Expose backoffMs/suspendedUntil/rateLimited in cache-status diagnostics

narrative.Action: In getDiagnostics (issue-data-service.ts:550-564), for each tracker add: backoffMs = cache.getBackoffMs(tracker, POLL_INTERVALS[tracker].default) (0 if no intervals entry); suspendMs = cache.getSuspensionMs(tracker); suspendedUntil = suspendMs > 0 ? new Date(Date.now()+suspendMs).toISOString() : null; rateLimited = suspendMs > 0. These flow through GET /api/cache-status (routes/misc.ts:1512-1515) unchanged.

items.title: getDiagnostics() returns, for each tracker, a numeric backoffMs, a suspendedUntil field (ISO string or null), and a boolean rateLimited.
Given 'linear' is exhausted (remaining:0, reset in future), the diagnostics entry returns rateLimited:true and a non-null suspendedUntil approximately equal to the reset time.

Given a tracker within limits, its diagnostics entry returns rateLimited:false and suspendedUntil:null while preserving the existing remaining/total/pollInterval/lastFetched/lastError/issueCount fields.
Add a pan doctor warn row for rate-limited / suspended trackers

narrative.Action: Add checkTrackerRateLimits() to doctor.ts following the getCachedIssueRowsForDoctor pattern (:226 — fresh `new CacheService()` reading the same SQLite DB). For github/linear/rally read getRateLimit + getSuspensionMs. Return status 'warn' (name 'Tracker Rate Limits') naming any tracker with getSuspensionMs > 0 (suspended, include reset time) or shouldBackoff true; otherwise 'ok' with message 'All trackers within rate limits'. Wrap in try/catch returning an 'ok'/skip result on DB error, mirroring existing checks. Wire it into the doctor check list next to the other tracker/cache checks.

items.title: Given the rate_limits row for 'linear' has remaining:0 and a future reset, checkTrackerRateLimits() returns status 'warn' with a message naming 'linear' and the reset time.
Given all trackers are within limits (or have no rate_limits row), checkTrackerRateLimits() returns status 'ok' with message 'All trackers within rate limits'.
`pan doctor` shows the 'Tracker Rate Limits' row in its output, and a CacheService/DB error produces a non-throwing ok/skip result (doctor never throws on it).
